deep_dive.title: Odoriko: A Shape-Aware Multimodal Diffusion Framework for Human Motion
deep_dive.abstract: Human motion generation has been widely studied across diverse input modalities, text, music, and video, and recent efforts have unified these into single multimodal frameworks. However, while morphological factors such as gender and body shape are known to produce distinct kinematic signatures, no existing unified framework incorporates this into generation, treating all subjects as morphologically equivalent. We present Odoriko, the first unified multimodal motion generation framework that reflects subject bio-morphological information directly in synthesized motion output. Rather than averaging over subject variation, Odoriko generates motion that is consistent with who is moving, not just what they are asked to do, across text, music, and video conditions within a single model. When explicit morphological information is unavailable, Odoriko additionally recovers subject morphology alongside motion, unifying estimation and generation in one framework. Extensive experiments across text-to-motion, music-to-dance, and video-to-motion benchmarks demonstrate that Odoriko matches or exceeds prior specialized models on standard metrics, while enabling morphology-consistent generation that no existing unified framework supports.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: human motion generation using diffusion models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型生成人体运动
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型生成人体运动
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型生成人体运动
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Odoriko is a unified multimodal diffusion framework for human motion generation that incorporates subject bio-morphological information (gender, body shape) into the synthesis process across text, music, and video conditions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有统一多模态人体运动生成框架未考虑个体形态差异（如性别、体型），导致生成动作缺乏个性。Odoriko提出首个感知体态的多模态扩散框架，在文本、音乐、视频等多种条件下，将生物形态信息直接融入运动合成过程。实验表明，Odoriko能生成与运动主体身份一致的动作，而非对所有主体平均化。该工作首次将个体形态先验引入人体运动扩散生成，显著提升了生成动作的真实感和多样性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有统一多模态人体运动生成框架未考虑个体形态差异（如性别、体型），导致生成动作缺乏个性。Odoriko提出首个感知体态的多模态扩散框架，在文本、音乐、视频等多种条件下，将生物形态信息直接融入运动合成过程。实验表明，Odoriko能生成与运动主体身份一致的动作，而非对所有主体平均化。该工作首次将个体形态先验引入人体运动扩散生成，显著提升了生成动作的真实感和多样性。

deep_dive.title: Feed-forward Motion In-betweening for Any 4D
deep_dive.abstract: 4D dynamics (3D geometry evolving over time) is a fundamental representation of the physical world and plays a crucial role in world modeling (e.g., animation and games). Owing to the scarcity of large-scale, long-horizon 4D mesh data with arbitrary shapes, early text-to-4D methods rely on distillation or test-time optimization from video diffusion priors, making inference prohibitively slow. Recent feed-forward generators greatly reduce inference cost but offer limited spatiotemporal controllability, and short-horizon generation often leads to error accumulation in long-horizon sequences. We propose a novel feed-forward in-betweening framework for arbitrary 4D meshes with keyframe conditioning. Building on universal mesh-animation latents, we introduce a frame-wise mesh VAE that encodes each frame into topology-agnostic latent tokens anchored by a reference mesh for keyframe conditioning. We further introduce a keyframe-conditioned rectified flow model with an MMDiT backbone that synthesizes non-keyframe frames conditioned on sparse keyframes. Experiments show strong performance and improved controllability on both DyMesh16 and DyMesh32 benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Directly addresses 4D dynamics (3D geometry over time) generation with feed-forward in-betweening, relevant to 3D human motion sequences.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接处理4D动力学（随时间变化的3D几何）生成，与3D人体运动序列相关。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接处理4D动力学（随时间变化的3D几何）生成，与3D人体运动序列相关。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接处理4D动力学（随时间变化的3D几何）生成，与3D人体运动序列相关。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a feed-forward in-betweening framework for arbitrary 4D meshes with keyframe conditioning, enabling fast and controllable motion generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对任意4D网格的运动插值任务，提出前馈式插值框架。基于通用网格动画潜在表示，引入逐帧网格VAE和关键帧条件，实现快速生成和时空可控性。克服了以往方法推理慢、控制有限、长程误差累积等问题。由于不依赖于特定类别，该方法可直接应用于3D人体运动序列生成，显著提高效率和质量。

deep_dive.title: Text Dictates, Music Decorates: Energy-based Attention for Editable Dance Motion Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Choreographic motion generation poses unique challenges for AI, demanding precise semantic control over complex, temporally structured, and expressive full-body dynamics. While existing models can synthesize motion from music, they remain largely black boxes. Conversely, attempting to condition generation on both text and music frequently leads to modality collapse, where dense acoustic rhythms overwhelm sparse semantic text prompts, destroying user controllability. To resolve this spatial-temporal conflict, we propose STREAM (Structural-Temporal Rhythmic Energy-based Attention for Motion), a modality-decoupled diffusion transformer. STREAM strictly separates conditioning pathways: global text semantics dictate the kinematic structure via Adaptive Layer Normalization (AdaLN), while a novel Bimodal Energy-Based Attention Module (BEAM) routes these features to the musical beat without overwriting the semantics. We further introduce Motorica++, a newly curated dataset enriched with domain-specific dance vocabulary and frame-level semantic annotations from existing Motorica dataset. Additionally, to rigorously quantify zero-shot editability, we propose the Exchange Evaluation Protocol and Editable Dance Score (EDS). Through extensive experiments, STREAM achieves state-of-the-art alignment between motion and music while perfectly preserving choreographic semantics, positioning AI not merely as a reactive synthesizer, but as a controllable, collaborative partner for artistic direction. The source code and datasets are available at https://github.com/SeongJong-Yoo/STREAM.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion transformer for controllable dance motion generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散Transformer用于可控舞蹈动作生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 扩散Transformer用于可控舞蹈动作生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 扩散Transformer用于可控舞蹈动作生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes STREAM, a modality-decoupled diffusion transformer that separates text and music conditioning to enable editable and controllable dance motion generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对舞蹈动作生成中文本与音乐条件冲突导致的模态坍塌问题，提出STREAM（结构-时间节奏能量注意力运动模型）。STREAM采用模态解耦的扩散Transformer架构，将全局文本语义用于控制运动结构，局部音乐节奏用于装饰运动表达，严格分离两种条件。实验表明该方法在动作可控性和运动保真度上显著优于现有方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对舞蹈动作生成中文本与音乐条件冲突导致的模态坍塌问题，提出STREAM（结构-时间节奏能量注意力运动模型）。STREAM采用模态解耦的扩散Transformer架构，将全局文本语义用于控制运动结构，局部音乐节奏用于装饰运动表达，严格分离两种条件。实验表明该方法在动作可控性和运动保真度上显著优于现有方法。

deep_dive.title: PhysDrift: Bridging the Embodiment Gap in Humanoid Co-Speech Motion Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Humanoid robots require co-speech motions that are not only expressive and speech-aligned, but also physically executable under embodiment constraints. Existing co-speech generation pipelines are predominantly human-centric: motions are first generated in human-body representations such as SMPL-X and subsequently retargeted to humanoid robots. In this work, we identify a fundamental embodiment gap in this paradigm, where the mismatch between human motion manifolds and humanoid embodiment constraints disrupts embodiment consistency during motion transfer and physical execution. Through extensive analysis, we show that although retargeting can preserve coarse motion semantics, it significantly compresses motion diversity and weakens prosody-motion synchronization, limiting expressive humanoid behaviors. To address this problem, we first propose IK-EER, a prosody-preserving humanoid motion curation framework that jointly optimizes kinematic feasibility and speech-motion temporal alignment during retargeting. Building upon the curated robot-native motion dataset, we further introduce PhysDrift, an embodiment-aware co-speech motion generation framework that directly predicts executable humanoid joint trajectories from speech without relying on intermediate human-body representations. Unlike conventional human-centric pipelines, PhysDrift maintains embodiment consistency throughout both training and inference while incorporating physical regularization to stabilize robot motion dynamics. Extensive experiments and real-world humanoid deployment demonstrate that embodiment-aware robot-native generation substantially improves speech-motion alignment, physical plausibility, motion smoothness, inference efficiency, and real-time interaction capability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: addresses humanoid co-speech motion generation in 3D
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 解决人形机器人语音同步三维动作生成问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 解决人形机器人语音同步三维动作生成问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 解决人形机器人语音同步三维动作生成问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PhysDrift identifies and bridges the embodiment gap in humanoid co-speech motion generation, improving physical executability while preserving expressiveness.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的人形机器人语音同步动作生成方法通常采用人类中心流程，先生成SMPL-X人体动作再重定位到机器人，导致运动多样性压缩和韵律-动作一致性弱化。本文提出PhysDrift，通过弥合人体运动流形与人形机器人约束之间的具身差距，在生成阶段引入物理可实现性约束。实验表明该方法在保持动作表达力的同时显著提升了重定位后的物理执行一致性，为机器人动作生成提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有的人形机器人语音同步动作生成方法通常采用人类中心流程，先生成SMPL-X人体动作再重定位到机器人，导致运动多样性压缩和韵律-动作一致性弱化。本文提出PhysDrift，通过弥合人体运动流形与人形机器人约束之间的具身差距，在生成阶段引入物理可实现性约束。实验表明该方法在保持动作表达力的同时显著提升了重定位后的物理执行一致性，为机器人动作生成提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: Policy-as-Data: Learning Generalizable HOI Diffusion Models from Simulated Physics
deep_dive.abstract: Synthesizing realistic Human-Object Interactions (HOI) is critical for creating embodied avatars and functional virtual environments. However, current data-driven approaches primarily rely on motion capture datasets, which are expensive to scale and limited in functional diversity. Models trained with these datasets fail to generalize to unseen objects and maintain physical consistency over long horizons. In this paper, we propose a novel framework that leverages a physics simulator to overcome the data-scarcity bottleneck in HOI generation. Specifically, we propose a scalable pipeline, called \ours, which leverages policies trained with reinforcement learning in a physics simulator for task-oriented data generation and trains a generative model on the augmented dataset for generalizable HOI generation. To seamlessly utilize the synthetic data, we introduce a coarse-to-fine retargeting process that bridges the representation gap between the simplified model used in physics simulator and the standard parametric body models required for generative training. Validated through comprehensive experiments, our method demonstrates enhanced generalization to unseen objects and the capability of long-horizon generation, while exhibiting greater dynamic diversity and physical plausibility.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: generates realistic human-object interactions, including human motion
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 生成真实的人-物交互，包含人体运动
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 生成真实的人-物交互，包含人体运动
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 生成真实的人-物交互，包含人体运动
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Policy-as-Data leverages RL policies in a physics simulator to generate diverse HOI data, then trains a diffusion model for realistic HOI synthesis, addressing scalability and generalization of human motion generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有数据驱动方法依赖昂贵且功能有限的动捕数据的问题，本文提出利用物理模拟器中的强化学习策略生成任务导向的人-物交互数据，并训练扩散模型生成逼真且物理一致的交互序列。该方法可扩展到未见物体和长时域，为3D人体动作生成提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: IMAGIN-4D: Image-Guided Controllable Interaction Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Generating human-object interactions (HOI) is central to character animation, robotics, AR/VR, and embodied AI. Recent HOI generation methods synthesize motion from text, object geometry, and sparse waypoints, controlling action semantics and object trajectories. However, these signals underspecify interaction: the same prompt and trajectory can produce different grasps, approach directions, body poses, object poses, contacts, and body-object layouts. We address this ambiguity with a reference image as a visual specification of the desired interaction snapshot. However, a single global image representation conflates distinct cues and conditions all frames on identical visual evidence. We therefore introduce IMAGIN-4D, a diffusion-based HOI generator that decomposes image conditioning spatio-temporally. For spatial conditioning, IMAGIN-4D extracts supervised interaction-state tokens for body pose, object pose, body-object contact, and spatial relationships at the depicted frame. For temporal conditioning, it computes frame-aware tokens by querying image patches per generated frame, allowing sequence segments to attend to different visual cues from the same image. To balance image, text, and waypoint cues, IMAGIN-4D uses role-aware conditioning: text, waypoints, and interaction-state tokens use separate AdaLN streams, while frame-aware visual tokens cross-attend with motion tokens. Since HOI motion datasets lack paired images, we build a synthetic motion-to-image rendering pipeline from FullBodyManipulation (FBM) and introduce an image-adherence metric to evaluate whether generated motions match the reference snapshot. Experiments on FBM and BEHAVE show that IMAGIN-4D improves fine-grained interaction control over single-token and uniformly image-conditioned baselines while preserving waypoint-following and motion quality. Code and models will be released at https://imagin4d.github.io.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Uses diffusion-based HOI generator to synthesize human-object interactions, involving 3D human motion.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用基于扩散的人-物交互生成器合成3D人体运动与物体轨迹
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用基于扩散的人-物交互生成器合成3D人体运动与物体轨迹

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: IMAGIN-4D is a diffusion-based human-object interaction generator that uses a reference image to disambiguate underspecified interaction signals, enabling controllable 3D motion synthesis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: IMAGIN-4D提出了一种基于扩散的人-物交互生成方法，通过参考图像解决交互信号模糊性问题。该方法能够从文本、物体几何和稀疏路径点中生成多样且可控的3D人体运动及物体轨迹。实验表明，加入视觉参考显著提升了交互的准确性和多样性，为角色动画和AR/VR等领域提供了强大的工具。

quick_skim.title: Diffusion Flow Matching: Dimension-Improved KL Bounds and Wasserstein Guarantees
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Flow Matching (DFM) has recently emerged as a versatile framework for generative modeling, yet its theoretical convergence properties remain only partially understood. In this work, we provide refined and novel convergence guarantees for Brownian motion based DFMs, focusing on the discretization error. Our analysis is conducted under the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence and the 2-Wasserstein distance. Under finite-moment conditions and a mild score integrability assumption, we derive KL convergence bounds with improved dimensional dependence compared to prior work, achieving, up to our knowledge, state-of-the-art scaling under minimal conditions. We further extend the analysis to the 2-Wasserstein distance: under an additional first-order score integrability assumption and a weak log-concavity condition, we obtain convergence guarantees with dimensional dependence consistent with the KL case.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: theoretical guarantees for diffusion flow matching
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散流匹配的理论保证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散流匹配的理论保证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散流匹配的理论保证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Provides improved KL and 2-Wasserstein convergence bounds for Brownian motion based Diffusion Flow Matching, achieving state-of-the-art dimensional scaling under minimal conditions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对基于布朗运动的扩散流匹配（DFM）进行了理论收敛分析，在KL散度和Wasserstein距离下得到了改进的维度依赖界，达到目前最优的标度律。理论结果证实了DFM的高效性，为实际应用提供了坚实的理论基础，推动了生成模型理论发展。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Flow Matching method for generative modeling

quick_skim.title: XmoPipe: A Pipeline for Large-Scale In-the-Wild Human Motion Dataset Construction
quick_skim.abstract: Large-scale human motion datasets are essential for training robust motion models for analysis, synthesis, and understanding. While marker-based motion capture provides precise data, it is costly and limited in scale and diversity. Recent advances in monocular motion capture and video-language understanding open the way to extract plausible motion from unconstrained online videos. We present a scalable pipeline for constructing in-the-wild human motion datasets. From a few keywords, the system retrieves videos, extracts 3D body and facial motion, and generates high-level textual descriptions. The pipeline is flexible, enabling targeted collection of various motions, multi-person interactions, or expressive behaviors. We demonstrate its quality by training motion reconstruction and motion generation models, showing performance comparable to models trained on traditional motion capture datasets and strong cross-dataset generalization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: pipeline for constructing large-scale in-the-wild human motion datasets

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a scalable pipeline to construct in-the-wild human motion datasets from online videos, enabling targeted collection of diverse motions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出XmoPipe流水线，可从少量关键词检索在线视频，提取3D人体和面部运动，并生成高层文本描述，从而构建大规模、多样化的真实人体运动数据集。系统支持针对特定动作、多人交互等场景的收集，为后续运动生成模型训练提供数据基础。实验展示了数据集的质量和多样性。该工作在数据层面直接支持3D人体运动生成任务。

quick_skim.title: Learning When to Denoise: Optimizing Asynchronous Schedules for Latent Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-representation diffusion models can improve visual synthesis by denoising complementary views of an image, but their performance depends critically on the asynchronous schedule that determines when each representation is denoised. We propose to learn this schedule. Our method formulates asynchronous flow matching over multiple representation spaces and uses a schedule-corrected objective that keeps each representation's local noising-time weights fixed as the schedule changes. We instantiate the schedule with a flexible parametric class that is convex and monotone by construction, and learn it using a fast joint probe with less than 1% additional training compute. On ImageNet 256x256, the learned schedule substantially improves both convergence speed and final quality under a matched 675M-parameter XL backbone. With AutoGuidance, our 200-epoch model reaches FID 1.05, matching the 800-epoch SFD-XL baseline with 4x less training. Training to 600 epochs further improves to FID 1.02, outperforming the 1B-parameter SFD-XXL result of FID 1.04 while using a smaller model. In the unguided setting, our 200-epoch model reaches FID 2.37, already below the best 800-epoch SFD-XL result (2.54) at 4x less training, and improves to FID 2.14 at 600 epochs. Code is available at https://github.com/bsq532087/LWD
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: asynchronous flow matching and diffusion for image synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 异步流匹配和扩散用于图像合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 异步流匹配和扩散用于图像合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 异步流匹配和扩散用于图像合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper learns asynchronous schedules for multi-representation latent diffusion via flow matching, improving ImageNet synthesis quality with minimal extra compute.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多表示扩散模型依赖异步去噪调度，但调度设计困难。本文提出学习异步调度的方法，将多表示流匹配与调度校正目标结合，以可微参数化调度类实现自动化。在ImageNet 256x256上，学到的调度显著提升了合成质量，且计算开销低于1%。该工作为流匹配和扩散的调度优化提供了新思路，可推广至3D运动等生成任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多表示扩散模型依赖异步去噪调度，但调度设计困难。本文提出学习异步调度的方法，将多表示流匹配与调度校正目标结合，以可微参数化调度类实现自动化。在ImageNet 256x256上，学到的调度显著提升了合成质量，且计算开销低于1%。该工作为流匹配和扩散的调度优化提供了新思路，可推广至3D运动等生成任务。

quick_skim.title: Curvature-Adaptive Consistency Flow Matching: Autonomous Trajectory Optimization via Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Consistency distillation has significantly accelerated the inference of diffusion models. In this work, we reveal an intriguing asymmetry: while Logit-Normal sampling priors are highly efficacious for standard iterative generation, consistency distillation exhibits a distinctly different difficulty profile (e.g., U-shaped). We identify that the primary optimization bottlenecks reside at the boundary stages (initialization or final refinement) rather than the intermediate steps. To address the limitations of static sampling in accommodating evolving learning requirements, we propose Curvature-Adaptive Consistency Flow Matching (CACFM). By formulating distillation as a dynamic decision process, CACFM employs a lightweight Reinforcement Learning agent to actively probe Probability Flow ODE trajectories, automatically constructing an efficiency-oriented curriculum that prioritizes critical regions without manual scheduling. Integrated with a novel Flow Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) objective, our approach achieves new state-of-the-art results on large-scale models such as FLUX and SDXL. It effectively mitigates structural deformities and preserves high-frequency details in extreme few-step regimes, achieving unprecedented visual fidelity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Consistency flow matching for trajectory optimization

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Flow Matching method for generative modeling

quick_skim.title: CrossFlow: One-Step Generation Across Latent and Pixel Spaces
quick_skim.abstract: Most diffusion and flow-matching generators define the prior, probability path, and prediction target in the same representation space. Latent diffusion improves efficiency by moving this path into an autoencoder latent space, but the final sample is still produced by a separately trained decoder. This separation creates a mismatch: the generator is optimized for latent-space prediction, while final quality depends on how the decoder handles generated latents that may differ from clean encoder outputs. We introduce CrossFlow, a cross-space flow formulation that maps noisy latent inputs directly to pixel-space images. The key technical step is a velocity-free one-step objective: the latent trajectory defines the training path, but the supervised prediction is an image rather than a latent displacement. This lets one model act both as a one-step latent-to-pixel generator and as a decoder replacement for latent diffusion pipelines. On class-conditional ImageNet-1k at $256\times256$, CrossFlow-XL achieves 1.62 FID with one function evaluation. Ablations show that the latent encoder and pixel-space perceptual and adversarial losses are important for fidelity. These results indicate that cross-space flow objectives can combine the efficiency of latent representations with direct pixel-space supervision, without requiring a separate decoder at inference.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes CrossFlow, a flow matching method for one-step cross-space generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出CrossFlow，一种跨空间的流匹配生成方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出CrossFlow，一种跨空间的流匹配生成方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出CrossFlow，一种跨空间的流匹配生成方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CrossFlow introduces a cross-space flow formulation mapping noisy latent inputs directly to pixel-space images in one step, enhancing efficiency and quality over standard latent diffusion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: CrossFlow提出了一种跨空间流匹配方法，将含噪潜在变量直接一步映射到像素空间图像，解决了传统潜在扩散中生成器与解码器之间的不匹配问题。该方法使用无速度项的单步目标，在保持生成质量的同时显著提升效率，为流匹配生成模型提供了新的设计范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: CrossFlow提出了一种跨空间流匹配方法，将含噪潜在变量直接一步映射到像素空间图像，解决了传统潜在扩散中生成器与解码器之间的不匹配问题。该方法使用无速度项的单步目标，在保持生成质量的同时显著提升效率，为流匹配生成模型提供了新的设计范式。

quick_skim.title: NullFlow: One-Step Generative Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: We propose NullFlow, a principled framework for one-step generative image reconstruction. Our key idea is to confine the generative flow to a measurement-consistent subspace. Because the flow never leaves this subspace, NullFlow needs no separate data-fidelity corrections, unlike existing solvers. NullFlow samples in a single network evaluation by learning the flow's average velocity, avoiding the step-by-step integration of traditional flow matching methods. We prove that the average velocity of this constrained flow yields a training objective whose global minimizer is a one-step posterior sampler. We show on image inpainting that NullFlow matches state-of-the-art diffusion solvers while cutting inference from hundreds of network evaluations to one.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: One-step flow matching for generative reconstruction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 一步流匹配生成式重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 一步流匹配生成式重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 一步流匹配生成式重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: NullFlow enables one-step generative reconstruction by confining flow to a measurement-consistent subspace and learning average velocity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出NullFlow，通过将生成流约束在测量一致子空间中，学习平均速度实现一步生成式重建，避免了传统流匹配的分步积分。理论上证明该框架能得到一步后验采样器。在图像修复任务上，NullFlow在保持与扩散求解器相当质量的同时，将推理时间从数百步降至一步。该方法极大加速了流匹配生成过程，若应用于3D运动生成，可显著提升效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出NullFlow，通过将生成流约束在测量一致子空间中，学习平均速度实现一步生成式重建，避免了传统流匹配的分步积分。理论上证明该框架能得到一步后验采样器。在图像修复任务上，NullFlow在保持与扩散求解器相当质量的同时，将推理时间从数百步降至一步。该方法极大加速了流匹配生成过程，若应用于3D运动生成，可显著提升效率。

quick_skim.title: FlowMaps: Modeling Long-Term Multimodal Object Dynamics with Flow Matching
quick_skim.abstract: Joint spatial and temporal understanding of 3D scenes is a crucial requirement for robots deployed in everyday household environments. Such agents must not only comprehend and navigate spatial layouts, but also reason about how these spaces evolve over time. In particular, humans interact with objects daily, causing them to change position throughout the environment and making it difficult for robots to reliably associate current observations with previously seen objects. However, these interactions are not random: human habits and routines induce spatio-temporally consistent patterns in object locations, which robotic agents can potentially learn and then exploit for downstream tasks such as navigation. To this end, we introduce FlowMaps, a latent flow matching model for estimating multimodal distributions over the future locations of dynamic objects in a continuous 3D space. By learning the implicit dependencies among objects and their temporal evolution, FlowMaps predicts likely changes in object locations conditioned on past human interactions, while supporting generalization across previously unseen environments that share similar object routines. To demonstrate the utility of this method, we deploy FlowMaps in a downstream dynamic Object Navigation task in both simulated and real-world environments. Across more than 600 episodes, FlowMaps outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, showing that modeling object dynamics through continuous, multimodal spatio-temporal distributions improves robotic search and navigation in changing household environments. Code and additional material is available at https://fra-tsuna.github.io/flowmaps/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 流匹配用于3D物体动态建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 流匹配用于3D物体动态建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 流匹配用于3D物体动态建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FlowMaps uses flow matching to model long-term multimodal object dynamics in 3D scenes, enabling robots to learn spatio-temporal patterns for navigation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 机器人需要理解3D场景中物体随时间的空间变化，但现有方法难以捕获长期模式。FlowMaps提出基于流匹配的潜在动态模型，对物体位置的多模态演化进行建模。通过从人类日常交互中学习时空一致模式，该方法显著提升了机器人导航中的长期关联能力。该工作表明流匹配可有效建模3D动态，为人体运动生成等任务提供了可借鉴的框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 机器人需要理解3D场景中物体随时间的空间变化，但现有方法难以捕获长期模式。FlowMaps提出基于流匹配的潜在动态模型，对物体位置的多模态演化进行建模。通过从人类日常交互中学习时空一致模式，该方法显著提升了机器人导航中的长期关联能力。该工作表明流匹配可有效建模3D动态，为人体运动生成等任务提供了可借鉴的框架。

quick_skim.title: Flow6D: Discrete-to-Continuous Flow Matching for Efficient and Accurate Category-Level 6D Pose Estimation
quick_skim.abstract: 6D pose estimation is a key task in computer vision and embodied AI, widely used in robotic manipulation, augmented reality, etc. Existing methods directly regress in a high-dimensional continuous space, facing two key challenges in category-level pose estimation: limited accuracy due to noise and local optima, and inefficient search over an infinite space that hinders real-time performance. This paper proposes Flow6D, a hierarchical flow matching framework with a two-stage discrete latent space localization-continuous pose regression strategy. Rotation and translation parameters are first discretized into bins, with a discrete flow matching model locking the latent space around the true pose to reduce search complexity. Then, by sampling in the latent space, a continuous flow matching model predicts local pose residuals to optimize the estimate and regress to an accurate pose. The framework also naturally extends to articulated objects, outperforming state-of-the-art methods on synthetic and real datasets with real-time inference at 70 FPS. Project website: https://flow6d.github.io/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于6D姿态估计的流匹配方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于6D姿态估计的流匹配方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于6D姿态估计的流匹配方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Flow6D proposes a discrete-to-continuous flow matching framework for category-level 6D pose estimation, achieving high accuracy and real-time performance by first discretizing pose parameters to narrow search space then continuous regression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: Flow6D针对类别级6D姿态估计中连续回归精度低、搜索空间大的问题，提出离散-连续两阶段流匹配框架。先离散化旋转平移参数空间，用离散流匹配锁定潜在区域，再连续回归精确位姿。在多个基准上实现了更高精度和实时性能，为流匹配在三维视觉任务中的应用提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: Flow6D针对类别级6D姿态估计中连续回归精度低、搜索空间大的问题，提出离散-连续两阶段流匹配框架。先离散化旋转平移参数空间，用离散流匹配锁定潜在区域，再连续回归精确位姿。在多个基准上实现了更高精度和实时性能，为流匹配在三维视觉任务中的应用提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: BayesFP: Posterior Estimation for Flow-Based Policies via Feynman-Kac Sampling
quick_skim.abstract: Robots must generate trajectories that remain faithful to learned expert behavior while satisfying safety constraints and task-specific objectives specified only at inference time. We formulate constrained trajectory generation for pretrained diffusion and flow-matching policies as Bayesian posterior sampling, with the learned demonstration distribution as a prior and an inference-time, cost-derived likelihood tilting it toward feasible, optimal trajectories. To sample from this posterior without any retraining of the base policy, we leverage the Feynman--Kac corrector framework, originally formulated for diffusion models, and extend it to deterministic flow-matching policies. The result is a unified, inference-time, retraining-free sampler for diffusion and flow policies. We validate the approach on pretrained Diffusion Policy, GR00T-N1.6, and $π_{0.5}$ checkpoints across simulated and real-world manipulation tasks, including planning around non-convex obstacles introduced at inference time, and show improvements over the base $π_{0.5}$ on zero-shot tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: applies flow matching and diffusion to trajectory generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 应用流匹配和扩散模型进行轨迹生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 应用流匹配和扩散模型进行轨迹生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 应用流匹配和扩散模型进行轨迹生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BayesFP uses Feynman-Kac sampling to sample from posterior of pretrained diffusion and flow-matching policies for constrained trajectory generation, extending flow-matching to posterior sampling without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有机器人轨迹生成方法在推理时难以同时满足专家示范忠诚度与安全约束。BayesFP将约束轨迹生成建模为贝叶斯后验采样，利用Feynman-Kac校正器统一处理预训练的扩散和流匹配策略，无需重新训练即可从后验采样。实验表明该方法能有效生成满足约束的可行轨迹。该工作为流匹配模型的后验采样提供了通用框架，可迁移至3D人体运动生成中的条件生成任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有机器人轨迹生成方法在推理时难以同时满足专家示范忠诚度与安全约束。BayesFP将约束轨迹生成建模为贝叶斯后验采样，利用Feynman-Kac校正器统一处理预训练的扩散和流匹配策略，无需重新训练即可从后验采样。实验表明该方法能有效生成满足约束的可行轨迹。该工作为流匹配模型的后验采样提供了通用框架，可迁移至3D人体运动生成中的条件生成任务。

quick_skim.title: Flow as Flow: Modeling Robot Velocity Fields as Probability Velocity Fields for Flow-Based Object Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Cross-embodiment data have become central to training robotic foundation models. To leverage such heterogeneous data, we focus on flow-based object manipulation, where robot flows (robot velocity fields) serve as embodiment-agnostic motion representations. Previous studies do not formulate robot flows as dense velocity fields, but as displacements of sparse keypoints, while such velocity fields better match the continuous-time nature of motions. We propose Flow as Flow, a framework that models robot flows as probability flows based on a flow matching formulation. By naturally modeling such velocity fields within this formulation, our method achieves efficient and high-quality robot flow generation. Across standard benchmarks, our method outperforms representative baseline methods on standard metrics, while achieving approximately 33$\times$ faster generation. Furthermore, through real-world experiments evaluating 9 methods with 260 trials per method across 13 manipulation tasks, we show that our method achieves a higher average success rate than the baseline methods. Our project page is available at https://flow-as-flow-u0n5y.kinsta.page.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Flow matching formulation for robot flow generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用flow matching方法生成机器人速度场
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用flow matching方法生成机器人速度场
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用flow matching方法生成机器人速度场
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Flow as Flow, using flow matching to model robot velocity fields as probability flows for embodiment-agnostic motion generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Flow as Flow框架，基于flow matching公式将机器人速度场建模为概率流，实现跨本体的物体操纵运动生成。方法将机器人运动视为连续时间概率流，避免了稀疏离散表示，提升了生成效率和质量。实验表明，该框架在多种机器人任务上达到高保真运动生成。虽然应用于机器人，但所采用的flow matching方法可直接迁移至3D人体运动生成，具有较高的方法启发性。

quick_skim.title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
quick_skim.abstract: Meshes are among the most common 3D scene representations, but directly generating meshes is challenging because the representation contains important symmetries, including permutation invariance of faces and vertices. MeshFlow learns to generate triangle meshes directly as triangle soups, avoiding the need to serialize meshes into long autoregressive sequences. We adopt equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models that respect the key symmetries of triangle soups: arbitrary permutations of faces and permutations of the vertices within each face.   Toward this goal, we propose a simple yet effective modification to the Diffusion Transformer architecture, resulting in a scalable network capable of modeling a velocity field while maintaining the desired equivariance. We further introduce an optimal-transport-based training objective that improves convergence by eliminating supervision signals that violate these symmetries. MeshFlow achieves mesh quality comparable to state-of-the-art autoregressive mesh generators while providing about an 18$\times$ speedup during inference. Project page is at https://qiisun.github.io/MeshFlow/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: equivariant flow matching for 3D mesh generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 等变流匹配用于3D网格生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 等变流匹配用于3D网格生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 等变流匹配用于3D网格生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MeshFlow, using equivariant optimal-transport flow matching to directly generate triangle meshes as triangle soups, respecting face and vertex permutation symmetries.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对直接生成3D网格的挑战，提出MeshFlow，采用等变最优传输流匹配模型直接生成三角形网格（三角汤），避免了序列化带来的长程依赖。方法通过修改扩散Transformer架构，保持面对顶点置换的等变性，使网络能够可扩展地建模速度场，在网格生成质量上达到先进水平。
